title: dendrosenecio kilimanjari subsp. cottonii
intro: Dendrosenecio kilimanjari subsp. cottonii, also known as the giant cotton bush or giant senecio, is a rare and endangered plant species native to Mount Kenya. The plant's leaves and stems contain the toxic compound pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions. The medicinal properties of the plant include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it useful in treating wounds, fever, and respiratory infections. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are often made into teas, infusions, or decoctions to treat various health issues, although its use is not recommended without proper medical supervision due to its toxic properties.
